workflow.name: Turn meeting recordings into Jira, ClickUp, and Linear tasks with Whisper and Claude
workflow.description:
This AI-powered workflow transcribes Zoom/Google Meet recordings, extracts decisions and tasks using AI, then creates tickets in Jira/ClickUp/Linear and assigns them to team members automatically.

### How it works

1. **Trigger** - Receives meeting recording URL via webhook or schedule
2. **Download Recording** - Fetches audio/video file from Zoom/Google Meet
3. **Audio Extraction** - Converts video to audio if needed using FFmpeg
4. **Transcription** - Uses Whisper API to transcribe meeting audio
5. **Wait & Process** - Allows transcription to complete
6. **Parse Transcript** - Cleans and formats the transcription text
7. **AI Analysis** - Claude extracts action items, decisions, owners
8. **Team Member Matching** - Maps names to user IDs in project tools
9. **Create Tasks** - Generates tickets in Jira/ClickUp/Linear
10. **Assign & Notify** - Assigns tasks to team members and sends notifications
11. **Meeting Summary** - Saves full summary to Google Drive/Notion
12. **Response** - Returns processed action items and task links

### Setup Steps

1. Import this workflow into your n8n instance
2. Configure credentials:
   - **Zoom OAuth** - For downloading Zoom recordings
   - **Google OAuth** - For Google Meet recordings and Drive storage
   - **OpenAI API** - For Whisper transcription service
   - **Anthropic API** - For Claude AI analysis
   - **Jira/ClickUp/Linear API** - For task creation
   - **Slack/Teams** - For notifications (optional)
3. Set up team member mapping in the config node
4. Configure your project management tool preferences
5. Activate the workflow

### Sample Trigger Payload
```json
{
  "meetingSource": "zoom",
  "recordingUrl": "https://zoom.us/rec/share/...",
  "meetingTitle": "Q1 Planning Meeting",
  "meetingDate": "2024-01-15",
  "attendees": ["alice@company.com", "bob@company.com", "charlie@company.com"],
  "projectKey": "PROJ-123",
  "taskTool": "jira",
  "defaultPriority": "medium",
  "autoAssign": true,
  "sendNotifications": true,
  "saveToNotion": false,
  "saveToDrive": true,
  "extractDecisions": true,
  "extractRisks": true,
  "dueDate": "2024-01-22"
}
```

### Features

- **Multi-platform support** (Zoom, Google Meet, MS Teams recordings)
- **Accurate transcription** using OpenAI Whisper API
- **AI-powered extraction** of action items, decisions, risks, and next steps
- **Automatic task creation** in Jira, ClickUp, or Linear
- **Smart assignment** - maps attendee names to task assignees
- **Meeting summaries** - saves comprehensive notes to Drive/Notion
- **Slack/Teams notifications** - alerts team members of new tasks
- **Duplicate detection** - prevents creating duplicate tickets
- **Priority detection** - AI assigns urgency levels to tasks

workflow.name: Meeting Notes to Action Items Automator

parameters.content:
## Meeting Notes to Action Items Automator

This AI-powered workflow transcribes Zoom/Google Meet recordings, extracts decisions and tasks using AI, then creates tickets in Jira/ClickUp/Linear and assigns them to team members automatically.

### How it works

1. **Trigger** - Receives meeting recording URL via webhook or schedule
2. **Download Recording** - Fetches audio/video file from Zoom/Google Meet
3. **Audio Extraction** - Converts video to audio if needed using FFmpeg
4. **Transcription** - Uses Whisper API to transcribe meeting audio
5. **Wait & Process** - Allows transcription to complete
6. **Parse Transcript** - Cleans and formats the transcription text
7. **AI Analysis** - Claude extracts action items, decisions, owners
8. **Team Member Matching** - Maps names to user IDs in project tools
9. **Create Tasks** - Generates tickets in Jira/ClickUp/Linear
10. **Assign & Notify** - Assigns tasks to team members and sends notifications
11. **Meeting Summary** - Saves full summary to Google Drive/Notion
12. **Response** - Returns processed action items and task links

### Setup Steps

1. Import this workflow into your n8n instance
2. Configure credentials:
   - **Zoom OAuth** - For downloading Zoom recordings
   - **Google OAuth** - For Google Meet recordings and Drive storage
   - **OpenAI API** - For Whisper transcription service
   - **Anthropic API** - For Claude AI analysis
   - **Jira/ClickUp/Linear API** - For task creation
   - **Slack/Teams** - For notifications (optional)
3. Set up team member mapping in the config node
4. Configure your project management tool preferences
5. Activate the workflow

### Sample Trigger Payload
```json
{
  "meetingSource": "zoom",
  "recordingUrl": "https://zoom.us/rec/share/...",
  "meetingTitle": "Q1 Planning Meeting",
  "meetingDate": "2024-01-15",
  "attendees": ["alice@company.com", "bob@company.com", "charlie@company.com"],
  "projectKey": "PROJ-123",
  "taskTool": "jira",
  "defaultPriority": "medium",
  "autoAssign": true,
  "sendNotifications": true,
  "saveToNotion": false,
  "saveToDrive": true,
  "extractDecisions": true,
  "extractRisks": true,
  "dueDate": "2024-01-22"
}
```

### Features

- **Multi-platform support** (Zoom, Google Meet, MS Teams recordings)
- **Accurate transcription** using OpenAI Whisper API
- **AI-powered extraction** of action items, decisions, risks, and next steps
- **Automatic task creation** in Jira, ClickUp, or Linear
- **Smart assignment** - maps attendee names to task assignees
- **Meeting summaries** - saves comprehensive notes to Drive/Notion
- **Slack/Teams notifications** - alerts team members of new tasks
- **Duplicate detection** - prevents creating duplicate tickets
- **Priority detection** - AI assigns urgency levels to tasks

parameters.text:
=You are an expert meeting analyst and project coordinator. Analyze this meeting transcript and extract actionable information.

**Meeting Details:**
- Title: {{ $json.meeting.title }}
- Date: {{ $json.meeting.date }}
- Attendees: {{ $json.meeting.attendees.join(', ') }}
- Duration: {{ Math.floor($json.transcript.durationSeconds / 60) }} minutes
- Word Count: {{ $json.transcript.wordCount }}

**Transcript:**
{{ $json.transcript.fullText }}

**Analysis Requirements:**

Extract and categorize the following in JSON format:

1. **Action Items** - Specific tasks that need to be completed
   - Task description (clear and actionable)
   - Assigned person (match to attendee list or extract from context)
   - Priority (high/medium/low based on urgency discussed)
   - Due date (if mentioned, otherwise null)
   - Context (relevant discussion that led to this task)

2. **Decisions Made** - Key decisions and agreements (if extractDecisions: {{ $json.config.extractDecisions }})
   - Decision description
   - Decision maker
   - Impact level (high/medium/low)

3. **Risks Identified** - Potential issues or blockers (if extractRisks: {{ $json.config.extractRisks }})
   - Risk description
   - Severity (high/medium/low)
   - Mentioned by whom

4. **Next Steps** - Follow-up meetings or milestones
   - Description
   - Tentative date
   - Owners

5. **Key Discussion Points** - Main topics covered
   - Topic
   - Summary
   - Time allocation (if determinable)

**Response Format:**
```json
{
  "actionItems": [
    {
      "title": "Clear, actionable task title",
      "description": "Detailed description with context",
      "assignee": "person@company.com or name",
      "priority": "high",
      "dueDate": "2024-01-20",
      "labels": ["engineering", "urgent"],
      "estimatedHours": 4,
      "dependencies": ["Other task if mentioned"]
    }
  ],
  "decisions": [
    {
      "decision": "Decision description",
      "decisionMaker": "Name",
      "impact": "high",
      "rationale": "Why this decision was made"
    }
  ],
  "risks": [
    {
      "risk": "Risk description",
      "severity": "medium",
      "mentionedBy": "Name",
      "mitigation": "Suggested mitigation if any"
    }
  ],
  "nextSteps": [
    {
      "description": "Follow-up action",
      "tentativeDate": "2024-01-25",
      "owner": "person@company.com"
    }
  ],
  "discussionPoints": [
    {
      "topic": "Topic name",
      "summary": "Brief summary",
      "timeSpent": "5 minutes"
    }
  ],
  "meetingSummary": "1-2 paragraph executive summary of the entire meeting",
  "attendeesPresent": ["List of people who actively participated"],
  "followUpRequired": true
}
```

**Important Guidelines:**
- Match assignee names/emails to the attendees list when possible
- If a task doesn't have a clear owner, set assignee to null
- Be specific in task titles - avoid vague descriptions
- Extract actual dates mentioned, or use null for due dates
- Prioritize based on language cues (ASAP, urgent, critical, etc.)
- Action items should be independently actionable
- Include labels that help categorize work (engineering, design, marketing, etc.)

options.systemMessage: You are an expert meeting analyst who extracts structured, actionable information from meeting transcripts. Always respond in valid JSON format without markdown code blocks. Be precise and thorough.

user.name: Oneclick AI Squad
user.bio: The AI Squad Initiative is a pioneering effort to build, automate and scale AI-powered workflows using n8n.io. Our mission is to help individuals and businesses integrate AI agents seamlessly into their daily operations  from automating tasks and enhancing productivity to creating innovative, intelligent solutions. We design modular, reusable AI workflow templates that empower creators, developers and teams to supercharge their automation with minimal effort and maximum impact.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
